creator: 000.fbsd@quip.cz
text:
(In reply to Dan Langille from comment #0)
Can you post separate output for kernel version and userland version tho be sure which is which?
freebsd-version -u
freebsd-version -k 

I am not running 12.x so I cannot test it at teh moment.

I don't know what can be wrong in your case. Version mismatch should not metter because kernel (version) and userland (version) are checked separately. Two idependent commands with two results:



if [ -z "`echo "$pkgargs" | egrep '^-[cj]'`" -a `sysctl -n security.jail.jailed` = 0 ]; then
        krnlv=$(freebsd-version -k | sed 's,^,FreeBSD-kernel-,;s,-RELEASE-p,_,;s,-RELEASE$,,')
        ${pkgcmd} audit $f $q $krnlv || { rc=$?; [ $rc -lt 3 ] && rc=3; }
fi

${pkgcmd} audit $f $q $usrlv || { rc=$?; [ $rc -lt 3 ] && rc=3; }
